text: Alif-L√£m-Ra . ňĻThis isňļ a Book which We have revealed to you ňĻO Prophetňļ so that you may lead people out of darkness and into light, by the Will of their Lord, to the Path of the Almighty, the Praiseworthy‚ÄĒ
Allah, to Whom belongs whatever is in the heavens and whatever is on the earth. And woe to the disbelievers because of a severe torment!
ňĻThey areňļ the ones who favour the life of this world over the Hereafter and hinder ňĻothersňļ from the Way of Allah, striving to make it ňĻappearňļ crooked. It is they who have gone far astray.

We have not sent a messenger except in the language of his people to clarify ňĻthe messageňļ for them. Then Allah leaves whoever He wills to stray and guides whoever He wills. And He is the Almighty, All-Wise.

Indeed, We sent Moses with Our signs, ňĻordering him,ňļ ‚ÄúLead your people out of darkness and into light, and remind them of Allah‚Äôs days ňĻof favourňļ.‚ÄĚ Surely in this are signs for whoever is steadfast, grateful.

ňĻConsiderňļ when Moses said to his people, ‚ÄúRemember Allah‚Äôs favour upon you when He rescued you from the people of Pharaoh, who afflicted you with dreadful torment‚ÄĒslaughtering your sons and keeping your women. That was a severe test from your Lord.
And ňĻrememberňļ when your Lord proclaimed, ‚ÄėIf you are grateful, I will certainly give you more. But if you are ungrateful, surely My punishment is severe.‚Äô‚ÄĚ
Moses added, ‚ÄúIf you along with everyone on earth were to be ungrateful, then ňĻknow thatňļ Allah is indeed Self-Sufficient, Praiseworthy.‚ÄĚ

Have you not ňĻalreadyňļ received the stories of those who were before you: the people of Noah, ‚Äô√ād, Tham√Ľd, and those after them? Only Allah knows how many they were. Their messengers came to them with clear proofs, but they put their hands over their mouths <sup foot_note=477>477</sup> and said, ‚ÄúWe totally reject what you have been sent with, and we are certainly in alarming doubt about what you are inviting us to.‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.477: This can mean that the disbelievers bit their own hands in rage, or they covered their own mouths mockingly, or that they put their hands over their messengers‚Äô mouths to silence them.

text: Their messengers asked ňĻthemňļ, ‚ÄúIs there any doubt about Allah, the Originator of the heavens and the earth? He is inviting you in order to forgive your sins, and delay your end until your appointed term.‚ÄĚ <sup foot_note=478>478</sup> They argued, ‚ÄúYou are no more than humans like us! You ňĻonlyňļ wish to turn us away from what our forefathers worshipped. So bring us some compelling proof.‚ÄĚ

foot_notes.478: It is out of Allah‚Äôs mercy that He does not hasten your punishment but delays your death until your destined time.

text: Their messengers said to them, ‚ÄúWe are ňĻindeedňļ only humans like you, but Allah favours whoever He chooses of His servants. It is not for us to bring you any proof without Allah‚Äôs permission. And in Allah let the believers put their trust.

Why should we not put our trust in Allah, when He has truly guided us to ňĻthe very best ofňļ ways? Indeed, we will patiently endure whatever harm you may cause us. And in Allah let the faithful put their trust.‚ÄĚ

The disbelievers then threatened their messengers, ‚ÄúWe will certainly expel you from our land, unless you return to our faith.‚ÄĚ So their Lord revealed to them, ‚ÄúWe will surely destroy the wrongdoers,
and make you reside in the land after them. This is for whoever is in awe of standing before Me and fears My warning.‚ÄĚ
And both sides called for judgment, so every stubborn tyrant was doomed.
Awaiting them is Hell, and they will be left to drink oozing pus,
which they will sip with difficulty, and can hardly swallow. Death will overwhelm them from every side, yet they will not ňĻbe able toňļ die. Awaiting them still is harsher torment.

The parable of the deeds of those who disbelieve in their Lord is that of ashes fiercely blown away by wind on a stormy day. They will gain nothing from what they have earned. That is ňĻtrulyňļ the farthest one can stray.
Have you not seen that Allah created the heavens and the earth for a reason? If He wills, He can eliminate you and produce a new creation.
And that is not difficult for Allah ňĻat allňļ.

They will all appear before Allah, and the lowly ňĻfollowersňļ will appeal to the arrogant ňĻleadersňļ, ‚ÄúWe were your ňĻdedicatedňļ followers, so will you ňĻthenňļ protect us from Allah‚Äôs torment in any way?‚ÄĚ They will reply, ‚ÄúHad Allah guided us, we would have guided you. ňĻNowňļ it is all the same for us whether we suffer patiently or impatiently, there is no escape for us.‚ÄĚ

And Satan will say ňĻto his followersňļ after the judgment has been passed, ‚ÄúIndeed, Allah has made you a true promise. I too made you a promise, but I failed you. I did not have any authority over you. I only called you, and you responded to me. So do not blame me; blame yourselves. I cannot save you, nor can you save me. Indeed, I denounce your previous association of me with Allah ňĻin loyaltyňļ. Surely the wrongdoers will suffer a painful punishment.‚ÄĚ
Those who believe and do good will be admitted into Gardens, under which rivers flow‚ÄĒto stay there forever by the Will of their Lord‚ÄĒwhere they will be greeted with ‚ÄúPeace!‚ÄĚ
Do you not see how Allah compares a good word to a good tree? Its root is firm and its branches reach the sky,
ňĻalwaysňļ yielding its fruit in every season by the Will of its Lord. This is how Allah sets forth parables for the people, so perhaps they will be mindful.
And the parable of an evil word is that of an evil tree, uprooted from the earth, having no stability.

Allah makes the believers steadfast with the firm Word ňĻof faithňļ <sup foot_note=480>480</sup> in this worldly life and the Hereafter. And Allah leaves the wrongdoers to stray. For Allah does what He wills.

foot_notes.480: That there is only one God worthy of worship.

text: Have you not seen those ňĻdisbelieversňļ who meet Allah‚Äôs favours with ingratitude and lead their own people to their doom?
In Hell they will burn. What an evil place for settlement.
They set up equals to Allah to mislead ňĻothersňļ from His Way. Say, ňĻO Prophet,ňļ ‚ÄúEnjoy yourselves! Surely your destination is the Fire.‚ÄĚ

Tell My believing servants to establish prayer and donate from what We have provided for them‚ÄĒopenly and secretly‚ÄĒbefore the arrival of a Day in which there will be no ransom or friendly connections.

It is Allah Who created the heavens and the earth and sends down rain from the sky, causing fruits to grow as a provision for you. He has subjected the ships for your service, sailing through the sea by His command, and has subjected the rivers for you.
He has ňĻalsoňļ subjected for you the sun and the moon, both constantly orbiting, and has subjected the day and night for you.

And He has granted you all that you asked Him for. If you tried to count Allah‚Äôs blessings, you would never be able to number them. Indeed humankind is truly unfair, ňĻtotallyňļ ungrateful. <sup foot_note=481>481</sup>

foot_notes.481: In this context, ‚Äúhumankind‚ÄĚ refers only to those who deny Allah‚Äôs favours.

text: ňĻRememberňļ when Abraham prayed, ‚ÄúMy Lord! Make this city ňĻof Meccaňļ secure, and keep me and my children away from the worship of idols.
My Lord! They have caused many people to go astray. So whoever follows me is with me, and whoever disobeys me‚ÄĒthen surely You are ňĻstillňļ All-Forgiving, Most Merciful.

Our Lord! I have settled some of my offspring in a barren valley, near Your Sacred House, our Lord, so that they may establish prayer. So make the hearts of ňĻbelievingňļ people incline towards them and provide them with fruits, so perhaps they will be thankful.
Our Lord! You certainly know what we conceal and what we reveal. Nothing on earth or in heaven is hidden from Allah.
All praise is for Allah who has blessed me with Ishmael and Isaac in my old age. My Lord is indeed the Hearer of ňĻallňļ prayers.
My Lord! Make me and those ňĻbelieversňļ of my descendants keep up prayer. Our Lord! Accept my prayers.
Our Lord! Forgive me, my parents, and the believers on the Day when the judgment will come to pass.‚ÄĚ
Do not think ňĻO Prophetňļ that Allah is unaware of what the wrongdoers do. He only delays them until a Day when ňĻtheirňļ eyes will stare in horror‚ÄĒ
rushing forth, heads raised, never blinking, hearts void.

And warn the people of the Day when the punishment will overtake ňĻthe wicked amongňļ them, and the wrongdoers will cry, ‚ÄúOur Lord! Delay us for a little while. We will respond to Your call and follow the messengers!‚ÄĚ ňĻIt will be said,ňļ ‚ÄúDid you not swear before that you would never be removed ňĻto the next lifeňļ?‚ÄĚ
You passed by the ruins of those ňĻdestroyed peoplesňļ who had wronged themselves. <sup foot_note=482>482</sup> It was made clear to you how We dealt with them, and We gave you ňĻmanyňļ examples.

foot_notes.482: Arab traders used to pass by the homes of some destroyed nations on their journeys to Syria and Yemen (such as ‚Äô»ād and Tham√Ľd, respectively), stopping shortly for rest.

text: They devised every plot, which was fully known to Allah, but their plotting was not enough to ňĻevenňļ overpower mountains ňĻlet alone Allahňļ.
So do not think ňĻO Prophetňļ that Allah will fail to keep His promise to His messengers. Allah is indeed Almighty, capable of punishment.
ňĻWatch forňļ the Day ňĻwhenňļ the earth will be changed into a different earth and the heavens as well, and all will appear before Allah‚ÄĒthe One, the Supreme.
On that Day you will see the wicked bound together in chains,
with garments of tar, and their faces covered with flames.
As such, Allah will reward every soul for what it has committed. Surely Allah is swift in reckoning.
This ňĻQuranňļ is a ňĻsufficientňļ message for humanity so that they may take it as a warning and know that there is only One God, and so that people of reason may be mindful.
